_comment: Ticker -> sector-neutral-ranking bucket (Phase 5 of the 5/10 -> 9/10 roadmap, see train.py::load_sector_mapping()/development/Changelog.md). Equities use GICS-like buckets; every bond ETF (Phase 1's sleeve) shares one 'Fixed Income' pseudo-sector and every crypto asset shares one 'Crypto' pseudo-sector, since neither has enough same-duration/same-coin members for a meaningful within-bucket rank - duration/credit distinctions are captured instead by features/macro_features.py's explicit macro proxies. Broad-market/international index ETFs (SPY/QQQ/IWM/EEM) share a 'Broad Market ETF' bucket rather than a single-company GICS sector, since they aren't one. AAA is intentionally omitted - an ambiguous legacy ticker, already observation-only (thin history, never trades) - load_sector_mapping() gracefully defaults any unmapped ticker to 'Unknown' rather than guessing.
